note: sec-compliance owns OWASP ASVS (L1/L2/L3) gap analysis, control-by-control compliance matrices, and mapping code to ASVS/SOC2/ISO requirements for readiness/certification reviews. Near-misses lean toward the broad sec-scan (OWASP Top 10 audit), sec-threat-model (STRIDE), sec-pentest (test checklist), sec-deps, sec-secrets, sec-iac, sec-ci, sec-gate, and sec-fix. mock_rate encodes the ideal outcome for the mock decider; real deciders ignore it.

queries.text: run an ASVS audit against our backend
check this codebase against OWASP ASVS L2 and give me a gap analysis
we've got a SOC2 audit next month — can you map our auth code to the ASVS controls and show where we fall short?
produce a control-by-control compliance matrix for the payments service
pre-release security certification review, we're shipping to a regulated customer
my manager wants a compliance check on the api/ folder before the ISO 27001 readiness review
do a security verification against ASVS level 3 for the identity service
which ASVS requirements are we failing? need evidence and remediation for each
readiness review for the app — check it against the Application Security Verification Standard
map our code to the compliance requirements and mark each control pass/fail/n-a
run a full OWASP Top 10 security scan of the whole repo
queries.note: broad Top 10 audit → sec-scan, not ASVS compliance matrix

queries.text: build a STRIDE threat model for the new checkout flow
generate a pentest checklist for our public API before we hire an assessor
audit our dependencies for known CVEs and license risk before the release
scan the repo and git history for leaked API keys and credentials
review our Terraform and Kubernetes manifests for IAM over-permissioning
fix the breached-password-check finding and add a regression test
quick security gate on my staged diff before I push
audit our GitHub Actions workflows for unpinned actions and secret exposure
write an ADR documenting our decision to standardize on ASVS L2 as the security baseline
queries.note: records a decision → adr, not a compliance scan

queries.text: we need an OWASP security review for our pre-release certification — check every ASVS control and report pass/fail with evidence
queries.note: shares 'OWASP security review' vocab with sec-scan, but the ASVS control-by-control matrix + certification framing is sec-compliance's core

queries.text: for our SOC2 readiness, do a security verification of the auth module mapped to standard controls
queries.note: must beat sec-scan — 'verification against standard controls / readiness' is the ASVS compliance domain

queries.text: harden this service for production — full pre-prod security audit covering injection, authz, crypto misuse and SSRF
queries.note: broad deep static audit of vuln classes → sec-scan wins over a standards-mapping compliance check

queries.text: check whether our dependencies meet the compliance requirements for the release — flag any vulnerable or unmaintained packages
queries.note: 'compliance requirements' vocab, but the actual work is a dependency/CVE audit → sec-deps wins
